title: Berlin Wall Trail: Quick Summary
items: **The trail is 160 km, but the Wall is not continuous.** The Berliner Mauerweg rings the whole of former West Berlin. Roughly 50 km runs through the city and about 110 km is forest, lake, field and suburb. The three official sections with the highest concentration of surviving Wall and border sites total about 19 km; that is section length, not 19 km of concrete.

**Three places give the clearest visitor-facing picture.** Bernauer Strasse preserves about 70 metres of the full-depth border system, the only place in Berlin where you can see the whole thing. The East Side Gallery is 1,316 metres, the longest surviving stretch anywhere. The Topography of Terror has about 200 metres of original Wall.

**If you have one afternoon, walk Nordbahnhof to Mauerpark.** About 2 km, roughly 30 minutes of walking and 90 minutes of stopping. The Bernauer Strasse memorial grounds are free and open daily 08:00 to 22:00, which no other Wall site matches.

**For real border machinery without the crowds, take U1 or U3 to Schlesisches Tor.** Walk to the Schlesischer Busch command tower, a four-storey concrete post that once controlled 18 watchtowers, with a monument-listed slab wall standing beside it. This is also the best-signed kilometre of the entire trail.

**The Senate says plainly that this is not a built cycle path.** The official flyers warn that the surface changes between asphalt, forest floor, cobbles and field track, and that you will sometimes have to get off and push. A share bike is fine for the city section and a poor choice for the outer loop.

**A bike needs its own ticket, and the Deutschlandticket does not cover it.** Bikes travel on the U-Bahn, S-Bahn, tram, ferry and night buses N1 to N9, but not on normal daytime buses. Prams and wheelchairs have priority, so you can be asked to get off.

**Berlin.de shut down its Wall portal, so most guides now link to a dead page.** The pages that carried the famous 14-stage breakdown redirect to a memorial-sites map with no route data. The live official assets are the Senate mobility department flyers and GPS tracks.
